channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Strategies for Sierra Leone to Ensure Mining Benefits Local Communities
item.description: Sierra Leone depends heavily on minerals, making almost 80% of its export revenue from mining. Despite this, many mining communities stay poor and often lose land because government decisions are made...

item.title: The Decline in International Student Enrolments in Australia
item.description: Australian universities have benefitted enormously from decades of international student enrolments, which have provided both economic and cultural advantages. The government first permitted public un...

item.title: Inside London Records: The Creative Freedom and Excess of a 90s Music Label
item.description: London Records, a label originally part of Decca Records and later independent under Polygram, became emblematic of 1990s British pop and dance music. Known for its edgy and diverse roster, including ...

item.title: ‘Elephants in the Fog’ Explores the Lives and Challenges of Nepal’s Kinnar Community
item.description: Abinash Bikram Shah’s feature debut, 'Elephants in the Fog,' offers an intimate and compelling portrayal of Nepal’s Kinnar community, legally recognized as part of the country's third gender. Set in a...

item.title: Sen. Ted Cruz avoids answering questions on January 6 rioters during TMZ interview
item.description: During an interview with TMZ reporter Charlie Cotton, U.S. Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX) faced repeated questions about whether January 6 rioters, some of whom were convicted and later pardoned, should be e...

item.title: 12-Year-Old Oyo Student Survives Terrorist School Attack by Hiding in Bush
item.description: Na serious wahala wey happen for Oriire Local Government Area, Oyo State, wey involve school attack by armed terrorists. Aminah, wey be 12-year-old pikin, talk say dem dey learn for classroom when gun...

item.title: Jigawa Government Secures Chinese Investment for Industrial Growth
item.description: Governor Umar Namadi of Jigawa State don confirm say him administration dey serious about creating correct environment wey go attract investors and improve industrial development for the state. Dis as...

item.title: Australia faces largest diphtheria outbreak in decades due to falling vaccination rates
item.description: Australia is currently experiencing its most severe diphtheria outbreak in decades, with nearly 220 cases reported across the Northern Territory, Western Australia, South Australia, and Queensland. Th...

item.title: Kehillat Israel synagogue reopens, offering hope to fire-affected Palisades community
item.description: Kehillat Israel, a synagogue in the Pacific Palisades, reopened last week for Shabbat services, becoming one of the first religious institutions to resume operations after the devastating fire that hi...

item.title: A Historical Look at Anna Pavlova Through Vintage Postcards
item.description: Anna Pavlova, born Anna Matveyevna Pavlova on February 12, 1881, was a legendary Russian prima ballerina known for her poetic expression and delicate frame. Rising to the rank of Prima Ballerina in 19...

item.title: Burt Reynolds: A Look at His Iconic Career and Style in the 1970s
item.description: Burt Reynolds, born Burton Leon Reynolds Jr. (1936–2018), was a defining figure in 1970s Hollywood, celebrated for his rugged charm, comedic timing, and physical performances. He rose to prominence th...

item.title: Xi and Putin Stress Strategic Ties and Criticize US Nuclear Policy
item.description: On May 20, 2026, Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a joint summit in Beijing, reaffirming their strategic partnership established in 2022. The leaders jointly crit...

item.title: Former Leader of Ex-Gay Ministry Arrested for Attempted Solicitation of Minor
item.description: Alan Chambers, 54, a former leader of the now-defunct 'ex-gay' ministry Exodus International, has been arrested in Florida for attempting to solicit sexual activity from someone he believed to be a 14...

item.title: Elon Musk Criticizes Casting of Lupita Nyong’o in ‘The Odyssey,’ Citing Personal Beliefs
item.description: Elon Musk has drawn attention for his prolonged criticism of Lupita Nyong’o being cast as Helen of Troy in Christopher Nolan’s upcoming adaptation of Homer’s 'The Odyssey.' Musk, along with conservati...

item.title: Canadian Opinions on Pipeline Investment Amid Alberta's Separatist Concerns
item.description: The Globe and Mail's letters to the editor for May 20 highlight a range of opinions on Canadian politics and energy policy, with a strong focus on Alberta's separatist tensions. Some readers question ...

item.title: China and Russia criticise US missile defence and nuclear policies, strengthen ties
item.description: In a significant diplomatic move, Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin jointly condemned US President Donald Trump’s plans for a missile defence shield and what they descr...

item.title: Tanzania Increases Defence Budget to Strengthen Borders and Modernise Military
item.description: Dodoma – Tanzania government imeamua kuongeza bajeti ya ulinzi mpaka 4.2 Trillion kwa mwaka wa fedha 2026/2027 ili kukabiliana na changamoto za usalama wa mipaka na kuendeleza jeshi. Waziri wa Ulinzi ...

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Bato dela Rosa’s Request to Block ICC Warrant
item.description: MANILA – The Supreme Court has turned down Senator Bato dela Rosa’s petition seeking a temporary restraining order (TRO) to stop the International Criminal Court (ICC) from enforcing its arrest warran...

item.title: Arsenal Clinches Premier League Title After 22 Years, Saka and Team Celebrate
item.description: Bukayo Saka don yan say Arsenal critics no dey laugh at dem again as dem don finally win Premier League after more than 22 years. Arsenal players and staff gather for London Colney training ground to ...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Moments of James Dean and Elizabeth Taylor’s Double on the Set of 'Giant'
item.description: During the production of the 1956 epic film 'Giant' in Marfa, Texas, photographer Frank Worth captured a series of candid, behind-the-scenes images featuring James Dean and Valley Keene, Elizabeth Tay...
